title: What are Java unnamed classes and instance main methods?
description: Explain the simplified Java entry point introduced in Java 21 preview.
options: JEP 463 allows writing Java programs without explicit class declaration or public static void main. Instance main methods: void main() {} — no args, no static. Unnamed classes: top-level methods and fields without class wrapper. Simplifies learning Java and writing scripts. The compiler generates the class implicitly
correct_answer: JEP 463 allows writing Java programs without explicit class declaration or public static void main. Instance main methods: void main() {} — no args, no static. Unnamed classes: top-level methods and fields without class wrapper. Simplifies learning Java and writing scripts. The compiler generates the class implicitly

title: What are Java string templates?
description: Explain the String Templates preview feature in Java 21.
options: String Templates (JEP 430) enable embedded expressions in strings safely. STR."Hello \{name}" processes the template with the STR processor. Unlike string concatenation, processors can validate/sanitize expressions. Built-in: STR (interpolation), FMT (formatting), RAW (unprocessed). Custom processors enable SQL injection prevention, HTML escaping etc.
correct_answer: String Templates (JEP 430) enable embedded expressions in strings safely. STR."Hello \{name}" processes the template with the STR processor. Unlike string concatenation, processors can validate/sanitize expressions. Built-in: STR (interpolation), FMT (formatting), RAW (unprocessed). Custom processors enable SQL injection prevention, HTML escaping etc.

title: What is the CSS color() function?
description: Explain wide-gamut color support in CSS.
options: The color() function enables wide-gamut colors beyond sRGB. Syntax: color(display-p3 1 0.5 0). Supports color spaces: srgb, srgb-linear, display-p3, a98-rgb, prophoto-rgb, rec2020, xyz. display-p3 covers 50% more colors than sRGB. Use with @supports and media queries (color-gamut: p3). oklch() and oklab() provide perceptually uniform alternatives
correct_answer: The color() function enables wide-gamut colors beyond sRGB. Syntax: color(display-p3 1 0.5 0). Supports color spaces: srgb, srgb-linear, display-p3, a98-rgb, prophoto-rgb, rec2020, xyz. display-p3 covers 50% more colors than sRGB. Use with @supports and media queries (color-gamut: p3). oklch() and oklab() provide perceptually uniform alternatives

title: What is the HTML Sanitizer API?
description: Explain the browser-native Sanitizer API for safe HTML.
options: The Sanitizer API provides browser-native HTML sanitization to prevent XSS. new Sanitizer() creates a sanitizer with safe defaults. element.setHTML(untrustedHTML, {sanitizer}) sanitizes and inserts. Configurable: allowElements, blockElements, dropElements, allowAttributes. Removes scripts, event handlers, and dangerous elements by default. No library needed
correct_answer: The Sanitizer API provides browser-native HTML sanitization to prevent XSS. new Sanitizer() creates a sanitizer with safe defaults. element.setHTML(untrustedHTML, {sanitizer}) sanitizes and inserts. Configurable: allowElements, blockElements, dropElements, allowAttributes. Removes scripts, event handlers, and dangerous elements by default. No library needed

title: What are MySQL common table expression (CTE) optimization strategies?
options: MySQL 8.0 materializes CTEs by default (computes once, stores in temp table). Non-recursive CTEs can be merged into outer query if referenced once (optimizer decides). Recursive CTEs always materialize. Use WITH to avoid subquery repetition. Optimization hints: /*+ NO_MERGE(cte) */ forces materialization; useful when CTE is expensive but referenced multiple times
correct_answer: MySQL 8.0 materializes CTEs by default (computes once, stores in temp table). Non-recursive CTEs can be merged into outer query if referenced once (optimizer decides). Recursive CTEs always materialize. Use WITH to avoid subquery repetition. Optimization hints: /*+ NO_MERGE(cte) */ forces materialization; useful when CTE is expensive but referenced multiple times

title: Write SQL to calculate the nth percentile using window functions.
correct_answer:
-- Calculate the 90th percentile salary per department
WITH ranked AS (
    SELECT 
        department_id,
        salary,
        PERCENT_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY department_id ORDER BY salary) AS pct_rank,
        CUME_DIST() OVER (PARTITION BY department_id ORDER BY salary) AS cume_dist
    FROM employees
)
SELECT 
    department_id,
    MIN(salary) AS p90_salary
FROM ranked
WHERE cume_dist >= 0.9
GROUP BY department_id;

-- Alternative: approximate using NTILE
-- SELECT department_id, MAX(salary) as p90
-- FROM (SELECT *, NTILE(10) OVER (PARTITION BY department_id ORDER BY salary) as tile FROM employees) t
-- WHERE tile = 9 GROUP BY department_id;

title: What is the difference between WebSocket and Server-Sent Events?
description: Compare SSE and WebSocket for real-time communication.
options: WebSocket: full-duplex, binary+text, custom protocol (ws://), both client and server push, requires upgrade handshake. SSE: server-to-client only (half-duplex), text only, uses HTTP (auto-reconnect, Last-Event-ID), simpler setup, works through proxies/firewalls. Choose SSE for notifications/feeds; WebSocket for chat, gaming, collaborative editing
correct_answer: WebSocket: full-duplex, binary+text, custom protocol (ws://), both client and server push, requires upgrade handshake. SSE: server-to-client only (half-duplex), text only, uses HTTP (auto-reconnect, Last-Event-ID), simpler setup, works through proxies/firewalls. Choose SSE for notifications/feeds; WebSocket for chat, gaming, collaborative editing

title: What is the difference between OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect?
options: OAuth 2.0 is an authorization framework (grants access tokens for API access, not identity). OpenID Connect (OIDC) is an identity layer ON TOP of OAuth 2.0 — adds ID tokens (JWT with user info), UserInfo endpoint, and standard scopes (openid, profile, email). OAuth = 'what can you access'; OIDC = 'who are you' + 'what can you access'
correct_answer: OAuth 2.0 is an authorization framework (grants access tokens for API access, not identity). OpenID Connect (OIDC) is an identity layer ON TOP of OAuth 2.0 — adds ID tokens (JWT with user info), UserInfo endpoint, and standard scopes (openid, profile, email). OAuth = 'what can you access'; OIDC = 'who are you' + 'what can you access'

title: What is Python's __all__ variable?
description: Explain how __all__ controls public API exposure.
options: __all__ is a list of strings defining which names are exported when 'from module import *' is used. If defined, only listed names are imported; if not defined, all names not starting with underscore are imported. Does not affect direct imports (from module import specific_name). Convention for declaring a module's public API
correct_answer: __all__ is a list of strings defining which names are exported when 'from module import *' is used. If defined, only listed names are imported; if not defined, all names not starting with underscore are imported. Does not affect direct imports (from module import specific_name). Convention for declaring a module's public API

title: What are JavaScript decorators (TC39 Stage 3)?
options: Decorators are functions that modify class elements at definition time. Syntax: @decorator before class/method/field/accessor. Receive context object with kind, name, addInitializer, access. Can replace methods, wrap them, add metadata, or run initialization code. @logged, @bound, @validate are common patterns. Different from TypeScript's experimental decorators
correct_answer: Decorators are functions that modify class elements at definition time. Syntax: @decorator before class/method/field/accessor. Receive context object with kind, name, addInitializer, access. Can replace methods, wrap them, add metadata, or run initialization code. @logged, @bound, @validate are common patterns. Different from TypeScript's experimental decorators

title: Write SQL to detect anomalous transactions using statistical methods.
description: Find transactions that deviate significantly from the mean.
correct_answer:
-- Flag transactions > 3 standard deviations from user's mean
WITH user_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        AVG(amount) AS avg_amount,
        STDDEV(amount) AS std_amount,
        COUNT(*) AS tx_count
    FROM transactions
    WHERE created_at >= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 90 DAY)
    GROUP BY user_id
    HAVING tx_count >= 10
),
flagged AS (
    SELECT 
        t.*,
        us.avg_amount,
        us.std_amount,
        ABS(t.amount - us.avg_amount) / NULLIF(us.std_amount, 0) AS z_score
    FROM transactions t
    JOIN user_stats us ON t.user_id = us.user_id
    WHERE t.created_at >= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 90 DAY)
)
SELECT * FROM flagged
WHERE z_score > 3
ORDER BY z_score DESC;

title: What is Python's __class_getitem__ method?
options: __class_getitem__(cls, item) is called when a class is subscripted: MyClass[int]. Returns a parameterized version (usually GenericAlias). Enables generic syntax without metaclasses: list[int], dict[str, int]. Custom classes implement it for type-hinting support: class MyList: def __class_getitem__(cls, item): return types.GenericAlias(cls, item)
correct_answer: __class_getitem__(cls, item) is called when a class is subscripted: MyClass[int]. Returns a parameterized version (usually GenericAlias). Enables generic syntax without metaclasses: list[int], dict[str, int]. Custom classes implement it for type-hinting support: class MyList: def __class_getitem__(cls, item): return types.GenericAlias(cls, item)
